transcript.clean_text: stage 19 of the tour de france 207 kilometers at a meeting of legends before the start yes you heard it i hope you win 35 stages today said the joint holder of 34. that was megs to cavendish would it end in a sprint though would it end in a breakaway an unpredictable day after hard days in the mountains with a tour de france all-time stage record up for grabs for the green jersey the first few kilometers showed a real battle to get up the road only eight out of the 23 teams participating had taken stage wins there was a crash in the first five k's though a couple of riders from the leader of the races team were caught up and tariq shot any other attacks down in the yellow jersey so six riders have got up the road and they established a gap of over four minutes once again to the intermediate sprint though things would change again the first six lots of points taken up by the breakaway michael matthews and sonny colbrelli taking a couple of points back on mark cavendish for camdish's lead still 35. and then the counter attacks came a real lack of control behind and the type of racing that we'd expected at the start over 20 riders getting involved in a counter attack a few of them being caught by the peloton behind the rest making a brand new group up front 14 riders plus the six already there made 20 behind complete apathy israel started nation chase for a little while but they couldn't bring it back no sprint today the gap will grow exponentially very quickly an opportunity gone for cavendish but the champs-elysees still remained and in the final 50 kilometers they attacked each other a stage went up for grabs for many for the first time others looking to repeat the feat a danger of missing the move and missing out mate maharish would go with 25 kilometers remaining already a stage winner at this tour de france and he quickly eeked out half a minute of an advantage again the chasing group behind would whittle down maybe a little lack of organization but simply no strength to catch the slovenian champion a third stage win for bahrain victorious at this race a second stage win at this tour for mate mahorich second place went to kofiris no first stage when in 13 years for them lapot just about getting the better of the man behind him casper piverson a reaction to the goings-on in the hotel with the police raid behind from mate maharich for the standings say morich beat laporte and peterson with turner sir and polit making up the top five stage 19 going to bahrain victorious maharich winning again it's time for the 14th time in his career and just over 20 minutes later the peloton came across the line yellow jersey belonging to today no changes in the overall pogaccha leading by 5 45 ahead of vinegar just six seconds between the dane and kara paz in third o'connor fourth keldam and fifth in the gc just the time trial still to go and that time trial takes the riders from lee bourne and will finish in saint-emilion a few twists and turns along the way and barring any strange you
